On August 27, 1896, the incredibly massive, devastating, and historically definitive Anglo-Zanzibar War officially and aggressively erupted. Heavily triggered by a massive succession dispute heavily interfering with British imperial interests, Royal Navy warships aggressively bombarded the absolute royal palace of Sultan Khalid bin Barghash. The incredibly massive, overwhelming British firepower completely destroyed the palace defenses in a mere 38 to 45 minutes, fundamentally securing its absolute, immortal legacy as the shortest recorded war in human history.

On August 27, 1979, Lord Louis Mountbatten, the massive, highly iconic final Viceroy of India and a deeply prominent member of the British royal family, was aggressively and brutally assassinated in Ireland. The Provisional Irish Republican Army (IRA) aggressively planted a massive, heavily concealed remote-controlled bomb on his fishing boat off the coast of County Sligo. The horrific, massive explosion tragically killed him and several others, aggressively escalating the massive, bloody sectarian conflict known as "The Troubles" and deeply traumatizing the British public.
